The '''superlative''' degree in Aelaki is formed through morphological '''umlaut''' — the fronting of vowels in the root. It applies to [[Adjective|adjectives]], [[Adverb|adverbs]], and other gradable forms.

== Formation ==

The superlative is created by applying vowel fronting (umlaut) to the vowels of the root template. Back vowels shift to their front counterparts.

== Examples ==

{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Base Form !! Superlative !! Part of Speech
|-
| ''koran'' "bright" || ''kœrœn'' "brightest" || [[Adjective]]
|-
| ''zadate'' "lengthy" || ''zædæte'' "lengthliest" || [[Adverb]]
|}

== Related Degrees ==

* '''[[Positive]]''': Unmarked base form
* '''[[Comparative]]''': Formed by C₂ reduplication rather than umlaut
* '''[[Negative]]''': Formed by /f/-infix

[[Category:adjective things]]
[[Category:git synced pages]]
